nativeRange.habitat: Vietnamese-collected trade cultivar (or cluster of cultivars) of Rotala. 'Vietnam' is widely used as an umbrella label by retailers for several Vietnamese-origin Rotalas, most often aligned with the Rotala rotundifolia complex, occasionally with Rotala wallichii.

decorPreferences: Nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) plus iron-rich root tabs
High light (PAR 50+) to push red/orange top color
CO2 injection strongly recommended for tight bushy form and committed color
Soft, slightly acidic water deepens red coloration
Hedge-trim regularly to maintain a dense colored carpet of tops

summary: Vietnamese trade cultivar (or cluster of cultivars) of Rotala from the rotundifolia complex, prized for orange-to-deep-red top growth under high-tech conditions. Treated by many retailers as a broader umbrella for Vietnamese Rotalas — often overlapping with the more specific 'H'Ra' selection — and demanding strong light, CO2, and iron to express its signature color.
careNotes:
Rotala sp. 'Vietnam' is a Vietnamese-origin trade cultivar widely treated as belonging to the Rotala rotundifolia complex; the label 'Vietnam' is used by retailers as an umbrella for several Vietnamese Rotalas, with significant trade overlap with the narrower 'Rotala Vietnam H'Ra' selection (entry fw-plant-161). Some sources also align it with Rotala wallichii (entry fw-plant-163). Functionally it is a demanding red-color background stem.

Care tracks the high-tech Rotala playbook: high light (PAR 50+), CO2 injection, nutrient-rich aquasoil substrate, and heavy macro+micro dosing (iron especially) for committed orange-red top growth. Without CO2 the plant survives but stays olive-green, growth slows, and algae pressure rises against the strong light it needs. Soft acidic water deepens the color; harder neutral water shifts the cultivar toward pink-green tones. Hedge-trim regularly to maintain a tight carpet of red tops along the back glass; propagation is via stem cuttings and lateral shoots from the trimmed lower stems. Cross-references: fw-plant-046 (R. rotundifolia), fw-plant-160 ('Colorata'), fw-plant-161 ('H'Ra'), fw-plant-162 (R. macrandra), fw-plant-163 (R. wallichii).

NOTE: Lineage of the 'Vietnam' label is unsettled across the trade. Reviewer should decide whether to maintain a distinct 'Vietnam' umbrella entry alongside the narrower 'H'Ra' entry, or to merge/cross-link them more explicitly.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium — Rotala Vietnam
primary.notes: Confirms Vietnam origin, background placement, stem-cutting propagation, moderate-to-strong lighting preference. Trade page positions it as easy-to-moderate; we record 'advanced' because dependable red coloration in practice requires high light + CO2 + iron, matching the H'Ra parent selection.

additional.name: Enchanted Grow — Rotala Vietnam Care Guide
additional.notes: Confirms Lythraceae family, Southeast Asia native range, 22-28C temp, pH 5.5-7.5, gH 2-12, moderate-to-high light, CO2 recommended, nutrient-rich substrate, iron-driven red coloration, 12-20 inch (30-50 cm) adult height.

additional.name: ABC Plants — Rotala 'Vietnam H'Ra'
additional.notes: Confirms Vietnam native range, moderate-to-high light, CO2 'required for density and coloration', 22-28C, pH 6.5-7.5, gH 1-12, nutrient-rich aquasoil substrate, iron-heavy fertilization, midground/background placement, stem-cutting propagation. Treats 'Vietnam H'Ra' as the same trade entity as 'Rotala Vietnam'.
